title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) straight from your oven – a classic Chinese bakery treat!

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a quintessential treat from Hong Kong bakeries, famous for their distinctive sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Despite the name, there is no pineapple in the bun itself; it is all about that golden, crispy crust contrasting with the soft, fluffy bread underneath. Enjoy these warm, fresh from the oven, for a truly authentic taste of Chinese comfort.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine warm milk, 30g granulated sugar, and instant dry yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy. Add 250g all-purpose flour, salt, and the large egg (for dough). Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Add 25g softened unsalted butter and knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough rises, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream 60g softened unsalted butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk and milk powder (if using) until well combined. Gradually mix in 100g all-purpose flour and baking powder until a soft, pliable dough forms. Wrap it in plastic and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and divide the dough into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a smooth, round ball. Place the shaped dough balls onto a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving some space between them.

instructions.title: Apply the Topping
instructions.text: Take the chilled pineapple topping dough from the refrigerator and divide it into 8 equal portions. Flatten each portion into a thin disc, about 3-4 inches (8-10 cm) in diameter. Carefully place one topping disc over each bun dough ball, gently pressing it to adhere and conform to the shape of the bun.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Egg Wash
instructions.text: Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let them rise again in a warm place for another 20-30 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Just before baking, gently brush the surface of the pineapple topping with the beaten egg wash for a golden sheen.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Bake the buns for 15-20 minutes, or until the pineapple topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. The internal temperature of the bun should reach 90-95°C (195-205°F).

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, transfer the Pineapple Buns to a wire rack to cool slightly. These buns are best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven, when the topping is at its crispiest and the bread is softest. Serve plain or with a slice of butter inside for an extra treat.

tips: For an extra crispy topping, ensure your butter for the topping is softened but still cool, and avoid overworking the dough. Chill the topping dough well before use.
If the pineapple topping becomes too sticky to handle while shaping, dust your hands lightly with flour or chill it in the refrigerator for a few more minutes.
Pineapple Buns are truly best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven or conventional oven to revive their crispy topping and soft interior.
article:
**Unlock the Secret to Perfect Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) at Home**

There are few aromas as comforting and inviting as that of freshly baked bread, especially when it’s the iconic Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao. A staple in Hong Kong bakeries, dim sum restaurants, and Chinatowns worldwide, this beloved pastry captivates with its unique blend of a soft, fluffy milk bread base and a sweet, crumbly, cookie-like topping. Despite its name, the Pineapple Bun contains no actual pineapple fruit; its distinctive crackled, golden-brown crust simply resembles the skin of a pineapple. If you've ever dreamt of recreating this culinary masterpiece in your own kitchen, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step of making an authentic Pineapple Bun Recipe, ensuring delicious results every time.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Pineapple Bun, known in Cantonese as Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包), is much more than just a sweet bread roll. It’s a cultural icon, particularly in Hong Kong, where it’s often enjoyed for breakfast or as an afternoon snack with a cup of milk tea. The magic of Bo Lo Bao lies in its delightful contrast: a tender, slightly sweet yeast dough forms the heart of the bun, while a rich, sugary crust creates its signature "pineapple" appearance and irresistible texture. This topping, typically made from sugar, eggs, flour, and butter, bakes into a crumbly, often cracked layer that is sweet, buttery, and utterly addictive. The combination of the soft bread and the crispy topping is what makes it so distinct and beloved. While some modern variations include fillings, the classic Pineapple Bun is usually enjoyed plain, allowing the interplay of its two main textures and flavors to shine.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun Recipe requires two distinct sets of ingredients: one for the soft bread dough and another for the crispy pineapple topping.

**For the Milk Bread Dough:**
*   3 cups (360g) High-protein bread flour
*   1/2 cup (120ml) Warm milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1/4 cup (50g) Granulated sugar
*   1 large Egg, at room temperature
*   2 teaspoons (6g) Active dry yeast
*   1/2 teaspoon (3g) Salt
*   1/4 cup (57g) Unsalted butter, softened

**For the Pineapple Topping (Bo Lo Bao Topping):**
*   1/2 cup (113g) Unsalted butter, softened
*   1/2 cup (100g) Granulated sugar (or half powdered sugar for a finer crumb)
*   1 large Egg yolk, at room temperature
*   1 teaspoon Vanilla extract (optional, for flavor)
*   1 1/4 cups (150g) All-purpose flour
*   2 tablespoons (15g) Milk powder (optional, enhances richness)

**For the Egg Wash:**
*   1 large Egg yolk
*   1 tablespoon Milk or water

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Embarking on this Pineapple Bun Recipe journey is incredibly rewarding. Follow these steps carefully for the best results.

**Part 1: Making the Milk Bread Dough**
1.  **Activate Yeast:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm milk, 1 teaspoon of sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Combine Ingredients:** Add the remaining sugar, egg, bread flour, and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or in a stand mixer with a dough hook until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 8-10 minutes, or use a stand mixer on medium speed for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic. It should pass the "windowpane test" (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
4.  **Add Butter:** Gradually add the softened butter, kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is soft, smooth, and no longer sticky. This may take another 5-8 minutes.
5.  **First Proofing:** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.
6.  **Divide and Shape:** Gently punch down the dough to release air. Divide it into 8-10 equal pieces (about 60-70g each). Shape each piece into a smooth ball by tucking the edges underneath. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space in between.
7.  **Second Proofing:** Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise again for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

**Part 2: Making the Pineapple Topping**
1.  **Cream Butter and Sugar:** In a separate bowl, cream together the softened butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2.  **Add Wet Ingredients:** Beat in the egg yolk and vanilla extract (if using) until well combined.
3.  **Add Dry Ingredients:** Sift in the all-purpose flour and milk powder (if using). Mix until just combined and a soft dough forms. Do not overmix.
4.  **Chill (Optional):** If the topping dough is too soft to handle, wrap it in plastic and chill in the refrigerator for 15-20 minutes.

**Part 3: Assembling and Baking**
1.  **Preheat Oven:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
2.  **Prepare Topping Discs:** Divide the pineapple topping dough into the same number of pieces as your bread buns. Flatten each piece between two sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap, or roll it out into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter).
3.  **Assemble Buns:** Carefully peel a topping disc and place it over the top of each proofed bread bun. Gently press it down and shape it to cover most of the bun's surface.
4.  **Score (Optional):** Using a sharp knife or bench scraper, gently score a crosshatch or spiral pattern on the surface of the topping. This helps create the classic pineapple appearance and allows for even cracking.
5.  **Egg Wash:** In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolk with milk or water for the egg wash. Lightly brush the topping of each bun with the egg wash.
6.  **Bake:** Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown and the topping is crisp and cracked.
7.  **Cool:** Transfer the baked Pineapple Buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving bakery-quality Pineapple Buns at home is entirely possible with a few key considerations. Here are some pro tips to elevate your Pineapple Bun Recipe:

*   **Don't Rush Proofing:** Patience is key for light and airy bread. Ensure your dough doubles in size during both proofing stages. A warm, humid environment (like a slightly warmed, turned-off oven with a bowl of hot water) can help.
*   **Perfect Dough Consistency:** The bread dough should be soft, pliable, and slightly sticky after kneading, but not overly wet. Resist the urge to add too much extra flour, as this can lead to dry buns.
*   **Topping Temperature Matters:** Ensure your butter for the topping is genuinely softened, not melted. If the topping dough becomes too warm and sticky while you're working with it, pop it back into the fridge for a few minutes.
*   **Even Topping Application:** Aim for an even thickness when rolling out your topping discs. This ensures consistent baking and a beautiful, uniform crust on your Pineapple Bun.
*   **Scoring Technique:** When scoring, be gentle. You want to cut through the topping but not deep into the bread dough itself. The scoring helps create the characteristic cracks and allows steam to escape, preventing the topping from sliding off.
*   **Monitor Baking:** Ovens can vary. Keep an eye on your buns during the last few minutes of baking. They should be golden brown, but not burnt. If they're browning too quickly, you can tent them loosely with foil.
*   **Cooling is Crucial:** Always cool your Pineapple Buns on a wire rack. This prevents the bottoms from becoming soggy due to trapped steam.

## Serving Suggestions

The beauty of the Pineapple Bun lies in its versatility. While utterly delicious on its own, here are a few serving suggestions to enhance your enjoyment of this incredible Pineapple Bun Recipe:

*   **Classic "Bo Lo Yau":** The most iconic way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun is fresh out of the oven, sliced in half horizontally, and with a thick slab of cold butter (bo lo yau) inserted into the middle. The contrast of the warm bun and the melting cold butter is pure bliss.
*   **Breakfast Delight:** Pair your warm Pineapple Bun with a cup of authentic Hong Kong-style milk tea or strong coffee for a traditional Asian breakfast experience.
*   **Afternoon Tea:** It makes a perfect companion for an afternoon tea spread, perhaps alongside some savory dim sum items or other sweet pastries.
*   **With Savory Fillings:** Although the traditional bun is plain, you can find variations with scrambled eggs, ham, or even luncheon meat inside, making it a more substantial meal.
*   **Plain and Simple:** Sometimes, the best way to enjoy a perfectly made Pineapple Bun is simply on its own, allowing the soft bread and crispy, sweet topping to be the stars of the show.

## Variations and Substitutions

Once you've mastered the basic Pineapple Bun Recipe, feel free to experiment with variations and substitutions to suit your taste and dietary needs.

*   **Fillings:** While traditionally plain, adding fillings is a popular modern twist. Consider incorporating a dollop of custard cream, red bean paste, taro paste, or even a small amount of char siu (BBQ pork) into the center of the bread dough before the second proofing.
*   **Flavorful Toppings:** Infuse the pineapple topping with different flavors. Add a teaspoon of matcha powder for a green tea topping, or a tablespoon of cocoa powder for a chocolate version.
*   **Healthier Dough Options:** For a slightly healthier take, you can substitute a portion of the all-purpose flour in the bread dough with whole wheat flour. Just be aware that this might alter the texture slightly and could require minor adjustments to liquid content.
*   **Vegan Pineapple Buns:** To make this Pineapple Bun Recipe vegan, use plant-based milk (like soy or almond milk), vegan butter, and egg substitutes. For the dough, a flax egg (1 tbsp ground flaxseed + 3 tbsp water) can work, and for the topping and egg wash, a commercial egg replacer or a mixture of plant-based milk and a pinch of turmeric for color can be used.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While more challenging due to the specific gluten structure required for soft bread, you can experiment with a high-quality gluten-free bread flour blend for the dough and topping. Results will differ from the traditional version, but it can be a good option for those with sensitivities.

Making Pineapple Buns at home is a delightful journey that culminates in a truly satisfying treat. This detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe provides all the guidance you need to bring the magic of Bo Lo Bao into your own kitchen. So gather your ingredients, clear your schedule, and get ready to enjoy the incredible aroma and taste of freshly baked Pineapple Buns!
